subjects.title: How to Think Clearly
subjects.blurb: The mental toolkit for spotting bad arguments, dodging your own biases, and reasoning your way toward the truth.

sessions.title: The lies your brain tells you
sessions.focus: how cognitive biases quietly distort everyday thinking, and why evolution built them in

sessions.title: Anatomy of an argument
sessions.focus: how to break any claim into premises and a conclusion, and tell a strong argument from a weak one

sessions.title: Spot the trick
sessions.focus: the most common logical fallacies and rhetorical sleights of hand, and how to name them

sessions.title: What actually counts as evidence
sessions.focus: correlation versus causation, sample size, and how to weigh a claim honestly

sessions.title: Thinking in probabilities
sessions.focus: why the world is uncertain and how to update your beliefs as new evidence arrives

sessions.title: The art of changing your mind
sessions.focus: steelmanning opponents, arguing in good faith, and treating being wrong as an upgrade

subjects.title: How We Know What's True
subjects.blurb: The greatest idea humans ever had — a reliable machine for turning guesses into real knowledge.

sessions.title: Before science
sessions.focus: how humans explained the world with myth and authority, and why that kept failing

sessions.title: The birth of the experiment
sessions.focus: how Galileo and others learned to ask nature direct questions instead of guessing

sessions.title: The heart of the method
sessions.focus: hypothesis, prediction, test — the loop that separates science from mere opinion

sessions.title: Why science can never prove it's right
sessions.focus: falsifiability, and why the best theories are the ones that dare to risk being wrong

sessions.title: How science polices itself
sessions.focus: peer review, replication, and why a single study is never enough

sessions.title: When science goes wrong
sessions.focus: fraud, bias, and the messy human reality behind the tidy ideal

subjects.title: The Universe's Story
subjects.blurb: From a fireball smaller than an atom to a hundred billion galaxies — the biggest story there is.

sessions.title: The night sky is a time machine

sessions.title: The Big Bang
sessions.focus: the evidence that the universe had a beginning, and what 'the beginning' really means

sessions.title: How to build a star
sessions.focus: how gravity turns clouds of gas into suns, and how suns forged the atoms in your body

sessions.title: Galaxies, black holes, and the dark stuff
sessions.focus: the vast structures of the cosmos and the invisible matter holding them together

sessions.title: How it all ends
sessions.focus: the far future of an expanding universe, and our place in deep cosmic time

sessions.title: The periodic table decoded

sessions.title: How atoms hold hands
sessions.focus: why atoms bond together to make everything from water to DNA

sessions.title: The dance of reactions
sessions.focus: how atoms rearrange to release energy, rust iron, and power your body

subjects.title: The Forces That Move the World

sessions.focus: the single most useful idea in physics — energy changes form but is never lost

sessions.title: The four fundamental forces
sessions.focus: the tiny handful of forces that quietly run the entire universe

sessions.title: When common sense breaks

sessions.title: Grasping deep time
sessions.focus: how mountains rise and erode, and how to truly picture a billion years
